name: From Coria del Rio promenade to La Isleta quarry - circular along the banks of the Guadalquivir River
name_original: Del Paseo de Coria del Río a la Corta de la Isleta — circular por la ribera del Río Guadalquivir
region_intro: Hiking around Coria Del Río offers diverse natural landscapes, significant waterways, and protected areas. The region is characterized by the Guadalquivir River, which forms a central feature with extensive riverbanks and associated vegetation. Hikers can explore wetland ecosystems within the Brazo del Este Natural Park and Mediterranean scrubland areas like Dehesa La Atalaya. These varied environments provide a range of terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Coria Del Río The most popular hiking route is From Coria del Rio promenade to La Isleta quarry - circular along the banks of the Guadalquivir River, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete, following the banks of the Guadalquivir River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Statue of Hasekura Tsunenaga loop from Coria del Río, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path that explores the local area, including the town's cultural landmarks. Local hikers also love the Palomares Sur Park loop from Palomares del Río, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through urban green spaces and surrounding areas, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Coria Del Río is defined by riverside paths along the Guadalquivir, wetland trails in the Brazo del Este Natural Park, and routes through Mediterranean scrubland. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Coria Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 1100 hikers have used komoot to explore Coria Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Coria Del Río?
A: There are over 80 hiking routes available around Coria Del Río, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 1100 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Coria Del Río?
A: Hiking in Coria Del Río offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find extensive riverside paths along the Guadalquivir, trails through wetland ecosystems in the Brazo del Este Natural Park, and routes traversing Mediterranean scrubland areas like Dehesa La Atalaya. This blend provides a mix of flat, riparian walks and slightly elevated scrubland paths.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Coria Del Río?
A: The trails in Coria Del Río are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic riverside walks, the tranquility of the natural parks, and the opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Coria Del Río?
A: Yes, Coria Del Río offers a good selection of easy trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. Approximately 37 routes are classified as easy, providing accessible options to enjoy the region's natural beauty.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes around Coria Del Río?
A: Hikes around Coria Del Río vary in length and duration. Many popular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1007869245/from-coria-del-rio-promenade-to-la-isleta-quarry-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-guadalquivir-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular route from Coria del Río promenade to La Isleta quarry&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 3 hours 50 minutes for a 9.3-mile (15 km) journey. Shorter options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19341943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Hasekura Tsunenaga loop&lt;/a&gt; are about 5.8 miles (9.4 km) and take roughly 2 hours 27 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Coria Del Río?
A: Yes, Coria Del Río features several enjoyable circular hiking routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1007869245/from-coria-del-rio-promenade-to-la-isleta-quarry-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-guadalquivir-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Coria del Rio promenade to La Isleta quarry - circular along the banks of the Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 9.3-mile (15 km) loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19341943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Hasekura Tsunenaga loop from Coria del Río&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.8-mile (9.4 km) moderate path exploring local landmarks.

Q: Can I see wildlife while hiking in Coria Del Río?
A: Absolutely. Coria Del Río is an excellent area for wildlife observation, especially birdwatching. The Brazo del Este Natural Park, a protected wetland, is home to diverse birdlife including swamphens, common storks, and various herons. The riverbanks of the Guadalquivir and its tributaries like the Guadaira and Pudio also provide habitats for migratory birds, hedgehogs, moles, and hares.

Q: Are there any interesting natural landmarks or viewpoints to discover on the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural points of interest. You can explore areas like El Bajo, a sedimentary beach on the Guadalquivir, or El Sotillo, an old meander that is now a tidal beach and bird habitat. The Dehesa La Atalaya features natural pine forests and wild olive trees, with a botanical route. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hut with views of the lagoon&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Dehesa de Abajo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What cultural or historical points of interest can I find along the hiking routes?
A: Coria Del Río has a unique cultural heritage. You can explore routes related to Hasekura Tsunenaga, a 17th-century Japanese samurai, and Blas Infante, the 'Father of Andalusian nationalism'. The town's Japanese legacy, including the Hasekura Tsunenaga statue, adds an interesting dimension to walks around the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19341943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Hasekura Tsunenaga loop from Coria del Río&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to combine hiking with cultural exploration.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Coria Del Río suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many trails in Coria Del Río are suitable for families. The diverse landscapes, especially the riverside walks and parks like the Carlos Mesa Gardens, offer pleasant and accessible environments for children. Look for the 'easy' rated trails for the most family-friendly options.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Coria Del Río?
A: While specific regulations can vary by protected area, generally, many outdoor areas and trails in Coria Del Río are dog-friendly, especially along the riverbanks and in less sensitive natural areas. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them to respect wildlife and other hikers.

Q: Is there public transport available to access the trailheads in Coria Del Río?
A: Coria Del Río is well-connected within the Seville province. Public transport, primarily bus services, can take you to the town center, from where many riverside trails and local loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1007869245/from-coria-del-rio-promenade-to-la-isleta-quarry-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-guadalquivir-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Coria del Rio promenade to La Isleta quarry&lt;/a&gt;, are easily accessible on foot.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Coria Del Río?
A: The best time to go hiking in Coria Del Río is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making long hikes less comfortable. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and lush vegetation, while autumn provides mild weather and beautiful scenery.
tips: Nice park where you can walk and eat in the establishments that are there. You can find tributes to Japan (which has a curious link with the municipality). It is located by the river, where the "Toro Nagashi" ceremony is held in summer.
Paddy fields where right in front you can see the island that is in the old middle of the Río Guadalquivir and that is called Isleta. The walk up to this point is quiet and beautiful!
Beautiful square that is in front of the Chapel of Nuestra Señora de la Soledad and where it contains the typical colors of this area of the province of Seville, white and yellow stripes.

waypoints.name: Plaza de la Soledad, Coria del Río
